title: GNN representation artifact plus alignment invariants

queries: graph masked autoencoder heterogeneous graph self supervised representation learning
graph contrastive learning preserve node identity transformed views
knowledge graph representation alignment invariant preservation sparse assignment

papers.title: GraphMAE: Self-Supervised Masked Graph Autoencoders
papers.claim: Masked feature reconstruction can learn transferable graph representations without downstream labels.
papers.relevance: The new GNN artifact should be a label-free graph-side module trained independently of Stage 3 task labels and reused during finetune.

papers.title: Heterogeneous Graph Masked Autoencoders
papers.claim: Heterogeneous graph pretraining benefits from attribute masking together with structure-aware objectives.
papers.relevance: The rel-amazon artifact uses heterogenous tables and directed FK relations, so masked node reconstruction plus edge prediction is the right medium-scale independent GNN objective family.

papers.title: GraphCL: Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning of Graph Representations
papers.claim: Contrastive agreement across transformed views preserves graph-instance identity and improves downstream transfer.
papers.relevance: The post-alignment entity-identity loss is the direct mechanism for preserving seed-entity identity when graph states are mapped into the LLM-aligned basis space.

evidence.why_now: Tier-3 review after EXP189/190/191 concluded that the cheap scorer and routing branch is saturated and that the missing signal must move upstream into representation quality plus alignment preservation. EXP108 showed item-incoterms is sensitive to preserving a stronger relational score surface, while EXP180 remained only retune-plausible and later routing follow-ups collapsed. The next justified move is therefore a larger but still coherent Stage3-compatible block: independent GNN representation training plus explicit alignment invariants during graph-to-token transfer.

hypothesis: If Stage 3 is currently losing salt-side ranking signal because graph structure is weakly represented before alignment and then over-mixed during basis transfer, then loading a label-free independently trained GNN representation artifact and explicitly preserving post-alignment invariants should improve cross-task stability without using the old --pretrain path. The key prediction is not immediate superiority on every task, but that item-incoterms can avoid the known collapse bands while throughput remains operationally acceptable under finetune-only training.
notes: Draft created after the Stage 3 architecture review moved the search away from small scorer/routing/distillation variants and toward larger pipeline blocks.
Keep Stage 3 representative experiments finetune-only; do not use --pretrain.
This candidate is the first integrated screen of Part 1 plus Part 3: load an independently trained GNN representation artifact and preserve relational invariants during alignment.
Artifact paths are dataset-bound: rel-amazon tasks must use artifacts/gnn_repr/rel-amazon/gnn_repr.pt and item-incoterms must use artifacts/gnn_repr/rel-salt/gnn_repr.pt.
Large-table graph cache may use multi-GPU row-sharded embedding, but aggregation, concat, and torch-frame materialization must stay CPU-backed to avoid repeat OOMs on review-scale tables.

Current rel-amazon artifact metadata is fixed at channels=256, num_layers=2, aggr=mean, so artifact-backed representative runs must align their GNN backbone shape to those values rather than the older per-task baseline defaults.
Do not relaunch retired manual scans or small scorer/routing neighbors around EXP189/190/191.

Implementation plan: keep the current basis-alignment path, load the independent GNN artifact through gnn_repr_artifact, add post-alignment basis-target retention, entity-identity contrastive pressure, branch orthogonality regularization, and sparse top-k basis assignment.
Stage4 compatibility story: the GNN artifact is label-free and reusable across databases, while the alignment invariants regularize transfer without introducing a target-only multiclass head.

Pre-launch gate requirement: run py_compile, git diff --check, candidate JSON/render validation, and a remote 64-step item-incoterms shape/throughput sanity check before launching the representative bundle.
Throughput should be judged primarily by per-GPU items/sec, i.e. visible train step/sec times per-rank batch size; aggregate items/sec and wall-clock are secondary operational context.

Expected failure signature: if item-incoterms still collapses into the known weak band or if per-GPU items/sec falls into an obvious long-tail regression, record the mechanism as blocked and retarget to the concrete bottleneck instead of launching the full bundle.

Phase 1 fixed-screen outcome was intentionally retained as retune_plausible rather than dropped, because user-churn screening already beat the fixed baseline, user-ltv subset MAE is only a continuation gate, and item-incoterms subset scores are known to be too optimistic relative to full test.
Phase 2 separate final-test-only confirmation completed on 2026-05-15 without rebundling Optuna and full test.

Phase 2 outcome is mixed but scientifically meaningful: EXP193 user-ltv beat the stored full-test reference with mae=15.633182804023788 and rmse=50.20323115209195; EXP194 item-incoterms beat the stored full-test reference with mrr=0.7254209687717201 and accuracy=0.6087479985602046; EXP192 user-churn remained below the stored full-test reference with roc_auc=0.6681287838379838 and average_precision=0.7236622468421053.

This mechanism family is therefore no longer only a placeholder retune_plausible direction with no final evidence: it now has two real full-test wins, including the key item-incoterms task, but it is not yet a clean representative-set sweep because user-churn missed its full-test reference by roc_auc delta -0.02367780299863631.

Program consequence after Phase 2: treat Part 1 as implemented and validation-backed enough to persist as the clean-worktree base for further work; do not rerun the same final-test-only wave again immediately; use this state to choose the next justified Part 3 follow-up or candidate-selection step.

final_results.consequence: Persist the EXP192 family as a mixed but scientifically meaningful Phase 2 result. Keep candidate status retune_plausible for program continuity, treat Part 1 as the validated base state, and move the next action to Part 3 follow-up selection or implementation rather than rerunning the same Optuna or final-test-only wave.
